reasoning: Without access to web search tools to retrieve stock index and individual mega-cap pricing data through October 26, 2025, I must rely entirely on base-rate estimates. For this question to resolve YES, at least four of the "Magnificent Seven" must close >50% below their all-time highs. While high beta names like Tesla and Nvidia have higher individual probabilities of enduring a >50% drawdown (due to highly cyclical demand and/or EV/AI hype cycles), a market dynamic pulling down four of these heavily-weighted, highly profitable stalwarts (especially Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, and Alphabet) would necessitate a severe, systemic macroeconomic crash comparable to 2008 or the 2000 Dot-com bust. The base rate for a crash of this magnitude occurring within any random ~2-year window (2024 to end-of-2025) is historically low, generally falling in the 5% to 10% range. Therefore, adjusting for slightly elevated bubble-bursting risks but overall strong mega-cap balance sheets, my probability estimate is 8%.
